summary: This is a brief link post where Joel Spolsky shares a Slashdot review of his book on user interface design. The review praises the book as a great primer for programmers who are intimidated by UI design. The reviewer highlights Spolsky's argument that programmers fear design because they see it as creative rather than logical. The review concludes that Spolsky successfully demonstrates UI design follows logical, learnable principles.
key_points: Programmers often fear UI design because they perceive it as a creative process rather than a logical one
Good user interface design is based on logical principles, not mysterious or indefinable magic
The book serves as a primer aimed at programmers with little design background
money_quotes: Spolsky feels that programmers fear design because they consider it a creative process rather than a logical one
The basic principles of good user interface design are logical and not based on some mysterious, indefinable magic
key_insight: User interface design is a logical discipline that programmers can learn, not a mysterious creative art to be feared.
